title: 🚀 Deployment Frequency [Edu] [v2]

panels.description: Data mode banner — shows whether this dashboard displays live GitHub data (green), synthetic seed data (amber), or demo data (blue).

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  CASE mode
    WHEN 'live' THEN '📡 Live Data — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    WHEN 'seed' THEN '🌱 Synthetic Seed Data — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    WHEN 'demo' THEN '🎮 Demo Environment — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    ELSE '❓ Unknown Mode'
  END AS "Data Source"
FROM data_mode
ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT 1

options.content:
# 🚀 Deployment Frequency

**DORA Pillar: Throughput**

## What it Measures
Deployment Frequency measures how often your organisation successfully deploys code to production. It is one of the two DORA *throughput* metrics and reflects the pace at which your team delivers value to users.

## Why it Matters
Higher deployment frequency indicates a mature, well-automated CI/CD pipeline and a team that delivers small, incremental changes. Elite-performing teams deploy on demand (multiple times per day), while low performers deploy less than once per month.

## How to Interpret
| Performance Level | Frequency | What it Means |
|---|---|---|
| 🟢 Elite | Multiple deploys/day (>7/wk) | On-demand delivery, small batch sizes |
| 🟡 High | 1–7 deploys/week | Regular cadence, good automation |
| 🟠 Medium | 1–4 deploys/month (<1/wk) | Larger batches, some manual steps |
| 🔴 Low | <1 deploy/month (<0.25/wk) | Infrequent releases, high risk per deploy |

panels.description: Deployment Frequency — DORA throughput pillar. Performance tiers and action guidance.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id)::float / GREATEST(1, EXTRACT(DAY FROM ($__timeTo()::timestamptz - $__timeFrom()::timestamptz)) / 7.0) AS "Deployments/Week"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
AND ds.state = 'success'
AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()

SELECT ROUND(
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN ds.state = 'success' THEN d.deployment_id END)::numeric * 100.0
  / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id), 0), 1
) AS "Success Rate (%)"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/deployments/deployments)

`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/deployments` + `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/deployments/{id}/statuses`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`deployments`, `deployment_statuses`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Counts distinct successful deployments divided by the number of weeks in the selected time window. Only deployments made via the GitHub Deployments API are counted — external CI/CD tools not integrated with this API are invisible.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id)::float / GREATEST(1, EXTRACT(DAY FROM ($__timeTo()::timestamptz - $__timeFrom()::timestamptz)) / 7.0) AS "Deployments/Week"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
AND ds.state = 'success'
AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
```

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/deployments/deployments)

`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/deployments/{id}/statuses`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`deployments`, `deployment_statuses`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Ratio of deployments with at least one `state='success'` status row to all deployments. The 'success' state is set by the API caller (deployer), not independently verified by GitHub. Deployments with no status rows sit in the denominator only.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT ROUND(
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN ds.state = 'success' THEN d.deployment_id END)::numeric * 100.0
  / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id), 0), 1
) AS "Success Rate (%)"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
```

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', d.created_at) AS time,
  COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id) AS "Deployments"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
  AND ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1

SELECT
  d.environment AS "Environment",
  COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id) AS "Deployments"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 2 DESC

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/deployments/deployments)

`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/deployments` + `/deployments/{id}/statuses`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`deployments`, `deployment_statuses`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Time series bucketed by `date_trunc('week', created_at)`. Weeks with zero deployments appear as gaps in the chart (no zero-fill). Timestamp is `created_at` (when deployment was requested), not when it completed.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', d.created_at) AS time,
  COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id) AS "Deployments"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
  AND ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1
```

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/deployments/deployments)

`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/deployments`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`deployments`, `deployment_statuses`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Bar chart grouped by the free-text `environment` field. 'production', 'prod', and 'PROD' are treated as three separate environments. No environment hierarchy — staging and production are weighted equally.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT
  d.environment AS "Environment",
  COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id) AS "Deployments"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 2 DESC
```

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', d.created_at) AS time,
  COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id) AS "Failed Deployments"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
  AND ds.state = 'failure'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1

SELECT
  d.sha,
  d.environment,
  ds.state AS status,
  d.creator_login,
  d.created_at
FROM deployments d
LEFT JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
ORDER BY d.created_at DESC
LIMIT 50

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/deployments/deployments)

`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/deployments/{id}/statuses`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`deployments`, `deployment_statuses`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Weekly count of deployments where any status row has `state='failure'`. Note: the API distinguishes 'failure' (ran but failed) from 'error' (couldn't start) — this query captures only 'failure'. Failed-then-retried deployments appear in both failure and success timelines.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', d.created_at) AS time,
  COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id) AS "Failed Deployments"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
  AND ds.state = 'failure'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1
```

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/deployments/deployments)

`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/deployments` + `/deployments/{id}/statuses`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`deployments`, `deployment_statuses`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Table showing one row per deployment with its most-recent status via LEFT JOIN. The `creator_login` is the account that called the Deployments API — often a bot or CI service, not the developer who wrote the code.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT
  d.sha,
  d.environment,
  ds.state AS status,
  d.creator_login,
  d.created_at
FROM deployments d
LEFT JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
ORDER BY d.created_at DESC
LIMIT 50
```

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', d.created_at) AS time,
  CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS "Cohort",
  COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id) AS "Deployments"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_pull_requests dpr ON dpr.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.number = dpr.pr_number
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
  AND ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Deployment Frequency: Copilot vs Non-Copilot - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/deployments/deployments)

`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/deployments` + `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`deployments`, `copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Weekly deployments split by whether the deployment `creator_login` holds an active Copilot seat. Built with a `WITH copilot_users AS (...)` CTE using a fixed 28-day window (`NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'`), independent of the Grafana time range.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', d.created_at) AS time,
  CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Copilot Active' ELSE 'Non-Copilot' END AS "Cohort",
  COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id) AS "Deployments"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_pull_requests dpr ON dpr.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.number = dpr.pr_number
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE ('$environment' = '__all__' OR d.environment = '$environment')
  AND ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY 1
```
